channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)

So much for blood being thicker than water.

61984164
>>61983393

61984176

61987080
>>61983396

Brown people use that word a lot without it actually being true. Usually it just means 'family friend' or 'childhood friend'.

61987372
>>61987349

61987679
>>61981230

61988313

predatory lending is most definitely a vibe in the USA

61988621

item.title: Day 1662 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 1662 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-03-15 22:41 UTC

OP

Day 1662 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Early replies (first 3)

61977222
bitcoin reaching 1 mil within a decade is honestly a conservative estimation. that shit's getting so scarce and there are no breaks on this train. volatility will dampen too so patience is the only winning strat.

61977235
>>61977222
But what if we... redistribute?

61977239
>>61977222
Magnificent trips

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61977388
>>61977222
> that shit's getting so scarce and there are no breaks on this train.

Scarcity != value retard.
You Mumus are so stupid its a wonder how you even solve the Captchas to make your dumbass post.

61977398
>>61977388
Hahahahaha just you wait another 1 year bearnegro

61977449
>>61977398
He's right. But you are also. Good times then?

61977594
>>61977398
>>61977449
>replying to himself
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAH
You forgot to change your VPN Moshe.

61977743
>>61977388
Then why are my rare Pepe's so valuable?
No, you can't see them

61977747
Pussys

61978226
>>61977204
cute

61978291
>>61977204
>>61977222

61978363
>>61977204
cute

61979178
>>61977204

61980756
>>61977388

61983374
>>61977204

61983392
I believe in you, as always

61987439
>>61977204

61988660

>legacy old fucking code, barely changeable
>miners requiring higher tx fees over time. who the fuck is gonna pay extra tx fees to make up for no new supply
gay

61984606

61988666

item.title: PREPARE FOR OIL TO BE $200 soon — 88 replies — 154.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
PREPARE FOR OIL TO BE $200 soon

Open thread • Replies: 88 • Updated: 2026-03-15 22:21 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61981675
>>61981670
How is trump's cult spinning this one?

61981683
we are a net oil exporter

61981696
>>61981675
high oil prices are good now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987829
>>61987761
>>61983908
Trump was making very consistent statements against Iran from long before he won the election. He's been shitting on Iran for decades, this was not expected.

You can make the argument that he shouldn't have interfered with Venezuela and be more correct than say he shouldn't have forced regime change on Iran.

61987831
>>61983239
>sieze a tiny island 4 miles off iran coast. Get droned/bombed/missiled 24:7.

If we do that then the islands biggest export wont be oil, it will be body bags. we will flee in a week

61987838
>>61987831
the USA has already demonstrated its vastly superior tech at this point, so long as the island isn't reachable by 1950s mortars/artillery they'll be safe enough at this point, so long as you have enough anti-missile interception tech.

The straights closed because you can get inbred sand niggers to hop on a jetski and drop a mine, which is a big fuckup by the navy that they haven't got effective anti-sea mine stuff at the moment. Someone said all the american navy minesweepers got decommissioned, bad idea.

61987842
>>61987838
>so long as you have enough anti-missile interception tech
they don't
same for anti-drone tech

61987845
>>61987831
perhaps that's what they want

anybody got that lord farquaad Trump meme

61987850
>>61981670
Always do the opposite of what /biz says

61987892
>>61981696
I've seen this before, and I'm not even 40.

61987937
>>61987065
You people don't know history. Saddam shut down that island 8 years. We couldn't hope to do the sort of damage to Iran that Saddam. He used sarin gas in cities, non stop airstrikes, bomb khark island so bad it couldn't function. And the told supported him, USA even bombed Iran in the 80s. Nothing mattered. The Iranians do not care. Shia Islam is a death cult. Your not shutting down the Iranian economy anymore than Saddam did for 8 years

61987942
>>61987937
The Iran Iraq war proved that as long as Iran can still make missiles and drones it will still fight. There is no "shutting down" of Iran. Any boots in a ground will just be a death sentence. Good luck hiding from 2 ton warheads on a tiny island 30 second flight from Iran. Kek.

61987944
>>61987206
they're already exporting nothing for the last 2 weeks+, Cuba is collapsing right now because its not getting any resources from anywhere else anymore.

>>61987207
it wouldn't be the worst idea, if you could figure out where the last of the iranian leadership is operating from.

61987945
>>61981670
Why was this post pruned? Seems significant.
>>61987782

61987955
>>61981675
Post again or you are a bot.

61987961
>>61987568
>Iranian media have dismissed Trump's claim that Khamenei is dead. Citing a source close to Khamenei's office, Iranian state media, IRNA, reported that the "leader of the revolution is steadfast and firm in commanding the field".
>Iranian news agencies Tasnim and Mehr also made similar claims, while Iran Times, citing sources, said Khamenei was "completely safe and sound".

61987970
>>61987838
Every based was overwhelmed in the middle east by drones. All CIWS and all patriots and thaads got blown out. The bases are effectively abandoned. Yet you think some low IQ Jarheads can storm a hostile island and establish an anti missile and drone bubble in the middle of hostile territory. K buddy

The marine general that gets that order, I hope they resign in disgust. Storming the island is easy . Sitting around being bombed is the issue

61987997
Why do we keep electing retards who can't help touching the fucking stove?

61988012
>>61987997
Because you are the retards, d'uh.
That's the best thing about democracy. The leaders are the reflection of the people.

61988014
>>61987997
Americans do not value intelligence, ignorance and acting without thinking of the consequences is a virtue to burgers

61988028
>>61981670
>OIL EXPECTED TO OPEN OVER $110

61988065
>>61981675
>How is trump's cult spinning this one?
Not very badly, approval rating is still hovering comfortably over 40%.

You got to understand that the average GOP voter doesn't care, doesn't understand, and doesn't care to understand what happens on the international scale. Trump could do the dumbest nigger move of all time and they would be okay with it cause they literally do not understand the ramifications. /biz/ cares cause despite being retarded, people here understand how interlinked international commerce is.

These people might wind up forgiving Trump even if gas goes up to $5 a gallon for the next 12 months.

61988130
>>61983213
>this is going to be 1929
probably worse

"bronze age collapse: oil edition" seems to be accelerating quite a bit.

61988156
OIL MARKETS OPEN IN 55 MINUTES

61988176
>>61983877
>Democrats were dead silent for 4 years while Biden sat on them
because they were trying to prosecute them retard, good luck doing that now. And they didn't release shit, they redacted the fuck out of anything incriminating so they could get everyone to onions out about fucking David Copperfield rather than the people who actually raped kids

61988180
>>61987829
>forced regime change on Iran.
where's the regime change? they haven't changed shit

61988294
>>61981675
Oil manufacturing will expand in America, also giving counterweight to China and the BRICS countries and any attempt to do BRICS shared currency, the petrodollar stays alive for a few more good years.

61988316
>everyone having a twitter politics brain instead of thinking about money

This board is shit. You guys being faggots about voting for zog puppet number one or zog puppet number 2 is beyond tiresome.

61988358
>>61988180
they've killed 2 leaders in a row and all top military officials on day 1, sure, its far from over, but the regime has certainly been 'changed'.

61988415
this is some worry bear garbage holy shit

61988425
It's over.

61988480
>>61988415
check the charts lol

61988532
>>61988358
It's still the same regime m8, same group same ways of thinking

item.title: >I work in tech >I make 7 figures a year >I - ACK Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily… — 62 replies — 131.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>I work in tech >I make 7 figures a year >I - ACK Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily…

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-03-15 22:27 UTC

OP

>I work in tech
>I make 7 figures a year
>I - ACK

Meta Platforms may cut up to 20% of its workforce as it spends heavily on ai.

Early replies (first 3)

61984851
Yea its because AI. Its not because they over hired and have to fire the entire VR dept.

61984853
>>61984829
Honestly, it has little to do with AI. You just don't need a lot of people to run a tech company regardless. Valve has 300 something employees for fuck sake.

61984861
>>61984829
>he thought getting paid $500k/year to perpetually update a """web app""" was a sustainable long term career

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987067
>>61986989
jobs wont really remain tho. AI companies will be nationalized in some form, because if they arent their existence doesnt make sense and will cause revolution, collapse, burning data centers, assassinations. so they will be nationalized, and everyone will be relegated to a UBI slave caste if they havent locked up hard assets, gotten a seat in the musical chairs in AI oversight, few trades jobs that robotics havent yet replaced, and then maybe some boutique luxury anachronistic jobs like extremely expensive Viennese coffee houses where people serve you. Dunkin donuts and shit like that or your hipster coffee shop probably wont make it. And most of the above only lasts for a few years. Youre about to see an insane restructuring of society in which status signaling fully replaces wealth. Hobbies and leisure will become everything and violently competitive post scarcity. Youre not gonna work on dunkin donuts and play vidya hoping it all blows over.

61987076
>2 more weeks.
Nothing ever happens.
LLMs have been stagnating for many months now. Just benchmarkmaxxing

61987078
>>61987076
youre ngmi

61987089
>>61986933
>>61987067
>>61987078
60-80 IQ speaks, it's time to listen.

61987106
>>61984829
Most of white collar jobs are made up bullshit larps. The fact that stupid shit like real estate agents still exist is a prove that not everything will be replaced and people will still larp in the future

61987125
>>61987106
Exactly. The need for work can be made redundant via technology, but the conditions for work can be replicated endlessly by the ruling class. There will be no catastrophic job market collapse. More and more labor will become superfluous as the market adapts to this new world.

61987169
>2024
>no I meant 2025
>its 2027
>uh fuck it, 2030...
>alright maybe 2050... please...

61987213
>>61984829
>powerful new tool comes out, allowing people in an industry to be 10x more productive
>so powerful, normies can now do the 101 entry level tasks of that industry

Normies
>"LMAO! TECH IS OVER! YOU DON'T NEED CODE TO MAKE A SMALL WEBSITE ANYMORE!!"

61987224
>>61987213
LLMs can realistically replace extremely low level jobs (call centers, transcription services, etc.) and codetrannies. they're worthless for basically anything else.

61987267
>>61984829
agi in 2 more gw sar

61987600
>>61987089
so what would you like to say anon? im listening

61987644
>>61987224
no wonder youre getting angry at conversation that goes over your head. youre an actual knuckle dragging retard. youre mistaking surface level retail facing LLMs that are essentially marketing/PR for what AI actually is, potentially. you will be angrily sniping at people who have legitimate even lukewarm IQ criticism for AI when its really here, youre a lemming

61987678
>>61987067
I’ll believe it when I see it. AI is fucking retarded still.

61987691
>>61987644
>>61987067
>more run-on gibberish sentences with bizarre syntax
ESL or learning disability, which is it? and how is it that an inferior nigger being such as yourself dares to lecture someone like me? have you absolutely no self awareness?

61987692
>>61984829
If you can't already afford your own personal Optimus Prime to program to fix your plumbing, it's ogre for you.

61987695
>>61987644
I work for a multi billion corporation that employs well over 250,000 people. We use Gemeni pro.

61987715
>>61984851
the guy who could only make shovel handles must have felt like a genius during the gold rush

61987734
>>61987691
>zero substance attack and illiterate fag cant get over me not using reddit spacing or something, has never encountered stream of consciousness posting on 4chan
go back

61987735
>>61987695
>>61987678
because youre a pig at an LLM trough and dont understand how incredible it is that even your LLM can write 1000 lines of flawless boiler plate code in under a second and pass the LSAT. and you dont understand the mechanisms behind that, and what agent swarms are, and what it means that this has all happened in 5 years and is getting exponentially better.
your enterprise llm subscription at some ancient boomer slop fortune 500 is completely irrelevant to what im talking about

61987742
>>61987734
>>61987735
are you the poorly medicated schizophrenic retard who brings up "analog world" nonsense when sufficiently provoked? go on, niggerbrain, say the line.

61987748
R/wF5FZB has to be a bot. There is no way that much nonsense comes from an actual human bean

61987751
>>61987742
keep going, im having a nice laugh at whatever sub 100 iq nigger goblin is writing this, calling discussion of a topic he doesnt understand or have the tools to comprehend calling it "bizarre syntax", "ESL" "schizophrenic".
youre just fundamentally incapable of engaging with something that I pretty clearly laid out. its ok that youre just not that bright
im sure all VC and world governments are misinformed and stupid. youre totally rational and correct here

61987757
>>61987751
how much do you make a year?

61987764
>>61987748
no, i think he's legitimately schizophrenic. when one of his mental health episodes completely spirals out of control, he goes on and on with massive walls of text >>61987742 about anti-LLM types being "analog."

61987771
>>61987757
more than you

61987776
>>61987764
>>61987748
the state of this website. abysmal. this place used to have fun and interesting 1st world people

61987783
>goes to discussion forum
>discusses on the forum
>anons get angry (??)
>maybe they have an interesting counter-take lets wait and see
>only intelligible criticism is i didnt use commas or something
>multiple people agree you are schizophrenic for engaging with the topic
actually the more i think about it, its always been like this

61987792
>>61987771
>>61987776
>>61987783
take your meds before the judge court orders a long acting anti psychotic injection. (you've been thorough all of this before, remember?)

61987799
>>61987792
ha ha

61988572
>>61987678
lmao

item.title: Bank Run ASAP — 42 replies — 72.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bank Run ASAP

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-03-15 22:20 UTC

OP

https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/boards-policy-regulation/us-feds-bowman-unveils-relaxed-bank-capital-rules-2026-03-12/

https://www.msn.com/en-us/money/markets/wall-street-banks-score-win-as-fed-unveils-plan-to-ease-capital/ar-AA1YuXLW

Early replies (first 3)

61981212
Nothing ever happens.

61981214
>>61981210
thanks i just deposited more into my bank

61981217
You think she's a good fuck?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61981333
>>61981306
The collateral to keep the ponzi afloat is gone. The road is gone, the can has fallen off a cliff, the trust is negative. The noose prepared, the mental preparation for bloody revenge internalized.

Let the bloodshed begin, may no banker, no polity, no bureaucrat former as current survive

61981365
>>61981333
>blocks your crash

TOO BIG TO FAIL

61981381
>>61981365
Not too big to be murdered

61981413
ngl I thought unc already had negative credit requirements

61981498
>>61981210
They did this the first week of lockdowns in 2020. Am I missing something?

61981557
>>61981498
Its attempting to signal
>we all under control
>member 2020
>how about buying bags
>you dont want to miss out
>p-please buy bag
But you know that, and your head comes off

61981560
>>61981498

Have you seen that national debt?

61981561
>>61981212
Until it does

61981567
>>61981560
the fed is certainly going to try to sully the sheets and by using up its powder preemptively bait a handful of retards to become exit liquidity. A shirt fart, a dead cat bouncing, and in late Q2/ early Q3 the main event, bankers, state level polity and bureaucrats heads on pikes, with the disembowled corpses of their families littering the streets

no mercy

61981572
>>61981217
She only takes anal because pussy is for her husband only.

61981585
>>61981210
The powers that be have a new system that has been in the development for decades now, ready to be rolled out in the coming years. It will operate in parallel starting sometime this year and by the next decade it will be fully operational as the Agenda 2030 goals are slowly being implemented.
There will be no collapse, no bank run, no violence, no uprising, no world war, no revolution, no killing of jews or politicians or other such fantasies. Instead there will be law & order, compliance and total surveillance until the very end. AI will be at the center of it all.

The Great Reset is inevitable.

61981618
>>61981262
They can lever up deposits even more? Like negative capital

61981623
>>61981217
She looks like she can suck the chrome off a trailer hitch

61981640
>>61981585
LMFAO
The test run was a total failure.
The "system" wont succeed just because propagandist, polity and millenarian geezers demand it.
The explosives and chemical warfare agents to commit a genocide are prepared.

The test run was a mistake, it provided enough time to prepare for the sustainable removal of 20th century artifacts in polity, bureaucracy and subsidized private epistemic communities

Kill yourself now

61982785
>>61981262
>>61981277
>>61981618
you guys don’t know what you’re talking about
capital requirements =/= reserve requirements

61983016
>>61981218
What does this mean
t. 88iq

61983038
>>61983016
Phenomenologically means things you perceive, see, experience. They really happen for you, they are real. Metaphysically means beyond the physical reality you or somebody perceives, sees, experiences, taken all together, nothing ever happens because from an ontological point of view, one that observes the entirety of reality and tries to fold it into causal and correlation relationships everything already happened

61983112
>>61981640

61983305
>>61981210
>>61981217
That is a man

61986997
>>61981210
>Aaron
I don't think so.

61987347
>>61981585
I'm pretty sure that violence is part of the plan.
That's why they mass import low IQ fighting age males.

61987358
>>61982785
Yeah no shit, hence why it collapses literally every retarded /pol/ faggots brain when you explain why banks still have to hold reserves despite the 0% requirement

61987382
>>61981217
nah she looks vanilla af

61987531
>>61981498
There are capital requirements and there are reserve requirements. Reserve requirements have to do with cash on hand to handle withdrawals and capital requirements have to do with balance sheet liquidity/solvency.

61987544
Bank runs have been patched out

61987571
>>61981210
>>61981212
>>61981214
>>61981217
The capital requirements have been 0 since 2020. So what the fuck are they talking about?

61987577
>>61982785
>>61987531
It doesn't matter. Go look at the capital requirements. That shit might as well be 0 too.

61987869
>>61981210
Man member when Jellybean Yellen forgot to refinance all the US debt and cost us like 2 trillion dollars?

61987978
>>61981262
They're just returning to that policy. QE but done through the banks instead of the fed.

61988525
>>61981381
glowing brightly moshe

item.title: BITCOIN IS WORTH A LOT BECAUSE... IT JUST IS OKAY??? — 15 replies — 39.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BITCOIN IS WORTH A LOT BECAUSE... IT JUST IS OKAY???

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-15 22:32 UTC

OP

bitcoin:
the good:
>OG nigga satoshi nakadashi made it and he was a cool british cryptographer lad (he pretended to be japanese and was active in british timezones)
>recognized as a CURRENCY by US government
>strongest p2p network (stable, reliable defense against attacks like sybil, eclipse etc)
>"brand" recognition
>vital for money laundering since it will never be illegal (too deeply invested in, atomic swaps and non-KYC)
the bad:
>slow fucking tx speed due to small mempool size: nobody is going to wait in the parking lot for 20 minutes to wait for the tx to confirm after you sell them a ps5. XRP is seconds.
>high tx fees that will get worse: no tail emission, no inflation. so tha tmeans miners need to charger more tx fees to make up for no coins being able to be mined anymore. besides that, it's already high as it is now
>only basic scripting? seriously? no smart contracts? the fuck, what fucking utility?
>traceable: great for governments. but makes your btc non-fungible because what happens if it traces back to a drug cartel LMAO. now you have tainted money, it cannot be treated like cash.

but yeah: high tx fees, slow uncomfortable confirmation and especially lack of scripting make it bad.

however, the one... BEST thing for bitcoin is that it is very good for money laundering. bitcoin has the strongest legal "foothold" in being hard to remove. So, it has use... But I'd like to see why people think bitcoin is #1 besides emotional sentiment. we all still love bitcoin but man, the utility barely checks out.

Early replies (first 3)

61983571
>>61983565
T
R
V
K
E

61983610
ITT: Dumbasses that don't know how the free market works.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61983616
>>61983560
dont you have an entire poorfag subreddit to seethe on since 2010 or so

61983635
>>61983560
>>61983565
>>61983571
>>61983610
>>61983616

And I am also in the thread

61983683
>>61983560
>>slow fucking tx speed due to small mempool size
you lost, altcoiners and bigblockers.

we have unlimited transactions per second on LN. suck it down.

61983793
>>61983610
(((Free market)))

61983859
>>61983560
I really need you guys to pump my vaporware up over $96,000 so that I can make my exit. you guys know how this works, so don't play dumb. I've done my part and it's time for you guys to step up and do yours.

61983956
>>61983560
>(he pretended to be japanese and was active in british timezones)
If someone says this you instantly know he's a normie that doesn't understand most programming side projects are done at night time.
All your other arguments also show a lack of understanding the basics.

61983967
>>61983560
Epstein said so.

61983991
>>61983560
>XRP is seconds
BOOOOOOOOOOOOM

61984005
>>61983793
Barely any crypto is worth any real money, maybe 5 of them but you had to get in early. BTC? Why, it's over $1000 a coin, normal ppl can't afford that, so how can anyone use it to buy normal things like groceries, like it was supposed to be used for?

61987478
>>61983560
what bitcoin maxi's wont accept is bitcoins position relative to all the other valuable assets out there. is it valuable, yes, does that value compare to other inherently valuable stuff, yes indeed. so at this point in time rather than is bitcoin worth something, the question is much more; do i want to buy 1 btc or rather a downpayment on a house, or half a shiny rock, or a portfolio in the (((stock market))) pick your poison

61988491
>>61983560
man you no coiners are salty

61988609
>>61983560
>recognized as a CURRENCY by US government

No cryptocurrency is recognized as currency. If you pay capital gains tax when you exchange it for goods or services that is by definition not currency. Bitcoin is recognized as a commodity, or property. This will literally never change unless governments one-day decide to act against their own economic interests (they will not do this.)

>slow fucking tx speed due to small mempool size: nobody is going to wait in the parking lot for 20 minutes to wait for the tx to confirm after you sell them a ps5. XRP is seconds.

Here is the big pill for you dumb XRP niggers to swallow. There isn't really a usecase for any crypto "currency" to be actually used as currency. Fiat currency works fine for day-to-day transactions unless there is Zimbabwe level hyperinflation and your paycheck is worthless a day after you receive it. People will continue to use crypto to circumvent online gambling laws or buy drugs, but that is all it has proven to be useful for currency-wise. Maybe blockchain tech will be utilized with stable coins but that is essentially just fiat currency on crypto rails.

The real problem with fiat currency is its inability to allow you to carry value through time. Gold has proven to be the best commodity to store value throughout history due to its scarcity and durability. Bitcoin is the evolution of gold, hence the moniker "digital gold." It solves all the problems of gold and improves upon it. That is the true value.

item.title: REMEMBER THIS THREAD — 22 replies — 26.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
REMEMBER THIS THREAD

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-03-15 22:05 UTC

OP

WASTE 95% OF YOUR WEALTH ON AN6THING NOW. IF YOU ARE RICH, THERE IS A REVOLUTION AGAINST THE RICH COMING...

Early replies (first 3)

61987006
>>61987000
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

61987019
>>61987000
Che...chee.. checked.

61987033
>>61987000
I wish. People are too brainwashed pr comfortable to give a shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987049
>>61987000
I wish but they'll just do another psyop to curve public attention

61987082
>>61987049
>>61987033
commie retards kys

61988073
>>61987000

There won't be a revolution. Men are too busy stabbing each other in the back to fight a common enemy. If they try, they will be crushed.

61988091
>>61987000
Lmao the copeunists are coping.
>reee I hate the rich because...
>uh why do I hate them again?
>oh yeah because I wanna be rich!!
>Reeeeeeeee
Shut up retard

61988103
>>61988091
how much do you make a year?

61988108
>>61987082
You are on the wrong side, goober.
Reconsider your views.

61988111
>>61988103
I make 696,969 a year selling dragon dildos to weak faggot communist fembois online

61988116
>>61988091
Start prepping and head for the woods

61988124
>>61988103
He isn’t poor. Thats for sure.

61988126
>>61988111
why don't poorfags ever answer this question?

61988132
>>61988124
it's hilarious that he thinks $700k/yr is rich. like that's his tongue-in-cheek response. >>61988111 i'm guessing he makes between $60k and $80k a year IRL.

61988144
>>61988126
For all you know my answer could be 99% true. For all you know, I really do make over 600k a year selling things to weak faggot commie fembois who are chronically online.
>>61988132

61988158
>>61988144
your fixation on 4chud memes and the "i've been found out, better attach an image to my post" behavior indicate otherwise.

someone who likely makes considerably more money than you is commanding you to answer a question. you'd better listen to your superiors.

61988165
>>61988158
Aww, that's cute. Your larp is funny. Someone's a little insecure about their wealth, aren't they?

61988211
>>61988132
Its definitely rich.

61988237
>>61988073
>>61987000
>>61987033

It's really all fantasy. Excluding a handful of high profile billionaires and celebrities you'd hardly know who to target. The rich are well protected in gated communities with private security and rapid police response if you did try to target big homes. Of course if you did target consumption the rich are quite capable of living with the appearance of restraint. I'm worth over $30MM and you couldn't pick me out as such most days

61988246
>>61988237
>the rich are untouchable gods!!! they are beyond any and all physical harm!!!!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V2iGJF0v0GI
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6sCioKnpHdY

61988409
>>61988237
> and you couldn't pick me out as such most days
Cause you don’t look good.
Growing up in a nice area, as kids, we would joke about richer kids and how richer kids looked different.

61988437
>>61988237
The only time rich people don’t look rich is when they are old.
Young rich people always look rich.

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 59 replies — 17.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-03-15 19:37 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61974399
>>61974366
Yes, they literally can. They can offer the exact same sales literally anyone else can. The waiver is literally pronouncement of this. This is SUCH a stupid aim at cope through SUCH a stupid argument. You are saving ZERO face and convincing no one with something this stupid.

61974410
>>61974383
>Eth literally had an ICO
And? As long as they didn't promise to make the price pump (like Ripple did) there's nothing wrong with that.

>>61974399
>The waiver is literally pronouncement of this.
The waiver grants Ripple use of Regulation D exemptions. No other crypto needs Regulation D exemptions to sell to institutions.

61974481
>>61974410
Lol And yet others who were were blocked and XRP fit Hinman's speech (which the SEC to tje judge's scorn withheld related discovery of well past the deadline) criteria much more than ETH for safe passage, already had clarity at FinCen (the US Treasury) and Ripple had consulted extensively with the SEC prior to the suit. See full timeline:
https://x.com/JohnEDeaton1/status/1494924825407668224

WAIVER CONDITION LITERALLY DOESN'T MATTER OR DISTINGUISH SALES. You are basically arguing that dad said Timmy couldn't go to the sleepover so he asked his mom and mom has veto power to the dad's determination so Timmy ended up being able to go. But you are Timmy's friend sitting there at the sleep over saying "nuh uh! Timmy can't REALLY go to the sleepover, his dad originally said he couldn't so he isn't really there like all the other kids! Such a low IQ argument it is actually literally unbelievable you think you are doing anything meaningful saying it. If it mattered at all Ripple would have not have dropped their appeal and they did drop it because it is functionally indistinguishable.

61974541
>>61974481
>WAIVER CONDITION LITERALLY DOESN'T MATTER OR DISTINGUISH SALES

The injunction only applied to direct institutional sales. The waiver means Ripple can use Regulation D exemptions from the injunction, implying a bunch of rules and restrictions that no other crypto has to abide by.
I'm done repeating myself.

61974584
>>61974541
Again, they literally can sell explicitly to the same people in exactly the same form of offering. You are coping INSANELY. Revisit the mummy sleepover veto analogy. Lol

61974603
>>61974584
Show us one other crypto that has to use Regulation D exemptions to sell directly to (institutional) buyers.

/discussion

61974686
>>61974603
Again, it is literally irrelevant and means absolutely nothing to sales or Ripple or institutions which buy. Lol But still, regulation D (particularly Rule 506(b) or 506(c)) is one of the most common U.S. securities law exemptions used by crypto and blockchain companies to sell their tokens/coins. It allows private placements to accredited investors (with no limit on the amount raised under Rule 506) without full SEC registration, often structured via Simple Agreements for Future Tokens. Ironic to your argument it became extremely popular to do to shift from uncompliant ICOs (like Ethereum's, which was MUCH more violative than XRP (which was not violative)) to private raises, especially for US investors. Filecoin raised $200M doing this. Kik Interactive, Blockchain Capital, and many others use and have used it to skirt regulation through enforcement and the ambiguity which has been exploited in bad faith in the past and is left open as a vector to the caprice of future admins which may be more adversarial. You don't even know what you don't know or how ironic your arument is. Pretty funny.

61974700
>>61970713
They deserve it and so much worse.

61978117
>>61974603
Well hello there biepum.
Hows that flippening coming along ?

61978659
>>61974686
You wanna know something really funny?
Every time Ripple wants to sell XRP to institutions, they would have to admit they're selling securities kek
See pic, it's the form they'd have to fill out.

Maybe that's why they haven't done it at all, and have been selling XRP exclusively to retail ever since the lawsuit and waiver.

61978914
how does the theory of all the XRP escrow is already alloted out to companies make sense if they sell like 1b XRP every month from there?

I know that most of it gets relocked, but assuming the 300m per month is avg, that means they sell 3.6b XRP per year. How long have they been selling that per year? How much was sold or locked away in 2018/2019/2020 etc?
Would that be sustainable if all of the escrow was already allotted to people?

Whats the maximum ratio of actual sales escrow to supposed locked/alloted escrow allowed to make this theory make sense?

61978921
>>61970713
Has the company/coin ever in its history done anything productive for society?

61979005
>Chainshitters absolutely seething over xrp
Yup, I'm thinking we're gonna wagmi really hard really soon

61979017
>>61979005
the thread was about Ripple, and this freak started manically fudding Link out of nowhere >>61972246

61979223
>>61979017
>out of nowhere
Kek, you'd had to have been living under a rock to not be aware that the majority of xrp seethe comes from mindbroken linkbaggies

61979295
>>61972246
all lies.
Ripple built their business around ledger that can be sold fitted to customer's needs.
XRP is just marketing product and IF someone would use XRPL it would use RLUSD or other stablecoin as gas.

61982361
>>61979295
RLUSD can't be used as gas, XRP is the only counterparty-less asset on ledger that will or can ever exist and is the most efficient and default used for auto-bridging between them, particularly between differently denominated assets, like bonds or stocks or tokenized moneymarkets or MPT tokens or stable dollars or stable yen or stable...etc. Lol You act like the stablecoin was a wrench in our thesis when OUR ENTIRE THESIS was LITERALLY ALWAYS that there would be hundreds of stablecoins and walled gardens and traditional and blockchain ledgers, which is literally the point. Ripple have acquired a broker dealer that processes trillions of dollars annually and is going to use XRPL for post-trade settlement. They are literally building an end-to-end institutional tech stack that uses XRP at every point. Their entire corporate action is basically building a business that brings XRP to corporate finance and function as broadly and deeply focused on liquidity base layers as possible and they have done an extremely good job doing so:
https://ripple.com/insights/institutional-defi-on-xrpl-scaling-real-world-finance-with-xrp-at-the-core/

Even if it makes you mad, this is inarguably the case.

61982372
>>61979017
You are either new or disingenuous. The same 2 or 3 retards make these threads to cope and get BTFO every single time. This was not a new and random mention and has years of precedent. They NEVER know how to defend their attacks or even their own technology, they literally just throw shit into the fan and get shocked and mad and very flustered and confused when it blows back in their face.

61982379
>>61978659
This literally isn't true. Any secondary programmatic sales (which is how they do almost all of their sales) are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings and XRP itself is the LITERAL ONLY other crypto to be declared a non-security officially except for BTC, regardless of what future admin decides. XRP is not a security, certain sales offerings are. Oranges are not securities, certain structured offerings of orange groves can be.

61983504
>>61982379
>Any secondary programmatic sales are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings
Yes, sales to retail via exchanges (aka programmatic sales) are not securities.

>which is how they do almost all of their sales
Exactly right, all of the XRP sold by Ripple is sold to retail.

This is exactly what I've been saying all along.
Glad you finally saw the light lmao

61983508
>>61982372
>Ripple triple dips by selling both tokens and stock, and then using the token sales to buy back the stock like the scammers they are
>"REEEEE FUCKING LINKIES AAAAA"

lmao

61985136
>>61983504
XRP is itself not a security and they literally haven't sold any to retail, they sell directly with buffer to institutional clients in a demand neutral way. Any crypto offering marketed in a particular way is a security offering. XRP is actually open to more forms of offering with clarity as a non-security because it is federally recognized as not being one (only other coin is bitcoin). Ripple can structure the sale to not be a securities offering VERY easily and have been doinig so throughout the suit over the last several years, but do not sell to retail and account for <1% of total volume which affects price less than a cent literally. Chainlink Labs dump directly on your head directly from node rewards anytime there is a 5% pump.
>>61983508
This is really deep cope. Lol You chose wrong-you are getting a whole nother attempt at choosing and buying with the dump that is happening/coming. Making it is more meaningful than signaling obsessive cult adherence to a fucking crypto project that was LITERALLY built to randomly insert rent seeking fees and solve ZERO problems and improve nothing of Chainlink Labs' products, who subsidize use and largely are used just for price feeds. You don't get anything by entombing yourself permanently in a corny culture of late normie greed fags hoping lightning strikes twice because you are incapable of forecasting trend. All you get is pissed off and carved out to being a really weird unlovable type of NPC. Choose to make it instead.

61985603
>>61985136
>they literally haven't sold any to retail
You just said yourself selling to retail ("programmatic sales") is how they do almost all of their sales.

>they sell directly
They can't. Not without filing a "notice of exempt offering of securities", which they have never done for any sale of XRP.
Probably because filing this document means admitting they're selling securities lol

61985792
>>61972246
>building an end to end tech stack that can establish XRP as the liquidity base of defi and the evolution of tradfi and the bridge between them
this has not been built
you're delusional

61985918
>>61970713
But mcap is 85 billion so they will just instant sell anything they buy for profit

61986807
>>61985792
>t-this has not been built
>y-you're delusional

61987872
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically through exchange through blind bid auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact >>61985792
It literally has. Hidden Road (now Ripple Prime) is a prime broker that handles back end for trillions in settlement annually with special emphasis in digital assets and privileged positioning for its build. GTreasury handles liquidity optimization and treasury management for thousands of companies and many Fortune 500s (bringing their idle capital onchain and moving through it), Metaco/Standard Custody for institutional grade custody used by many of the world's biggest banks already. They have a national bank charter with the OCC. Rail adds virtual accounts for collections and automated fiat-stablecoin conversion/settlement. Palisade gives wallet-as-a-service custody with MPC security, fast provisioning, multi-chain, and treasury automation for scalable fund sweeping. Their platform unifies collection, custody, exchange, settlement, and payout across fiat and digital assets in a single seamless API driven solution. Literal end-to-end solution doing exactly what I said and built piecewise with XRP at the heart of every single piece of it. Their corporate strategy is literally to drive XRP into the deep system of things by building the corporate architecture around it and having others enter it through them. Complete opposite to Chainlink who just shill to retards by publishing pictures of numbers that never translate to use which is only used as a jewish fee insertion for no reason. Pic related.

61987881
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically on exchange through blind auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact same amount purchases for institutional clients. Their net impact on the exchange is 0. They literally do this already and have throughout the lawsuit while they had the injunction limiting their sales. You are SO a disingenuous, low IQ cope clinging and desperate.

61987900
>>61970713
should I sell my chainlink?
I'm up ~3x still on dca but chainlinkfag keeps losing his mind over xrp and I'm thinking it must be over for link

61987906
>>61987872
>>61987881
Yes, Ripple has been selling XRP exclusively through blind sales via exchanges ("programmatic sales"). In other words: to retail.

This is the third time you're agreeing with me on the same exact thing.
Are you sure you're ok?

item.title: What should I do with 50k? — 19 replies — 17.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
What should I do with 50k?

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-15 21:50 UTC

OP

I've got 50k in a savings account and about 15k in the market (VOO, etc), do I keep DCAing? What if the market crashes?

Early replies (first 3)

61987565
I’d keep it exactly as is for now. We should get a much bigger pullback in stocks this year from the unfolding liquidity crisis.

61987573
>>61987552
Buy a few oz of silver just in case.

61987574
>>61987552
Do you have anything of value to offer to the world? If so, invest in that.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987576
>>61987552
Wait a few more weeks for the big economic downturn and then invest it all in a Global Index fund.

61987604
>>61987552
>do I keep DCAing?
yes
>What if the market crashes
a market crash is also called "a sale". You keep DCAing. Unless you prefer to buy high instead of buying low, in which case knock yourself out.

61987619
the worst thing you can do is let it sit there doing nothing

61987723
>>61987552
Wait for the turbo crash and put at least $1k into XRP and hold it like a savings account for a long time.
>>61987619
This is definitely not the worst thing he can do if markets are set to crash in unison. Lol Stop paying and listening to a financial advisor who is paid a fee to buy you index funds and still underperforms the market.

61987727
>>61987552
>50k in a savings account
ok so you invest it and either
>nothing happens and you make a modest 7-15%
>something happens and market collapses and fed actions cause sharp inflation and you lose 7-8% anyway

61987738
>>61987565
people said this at the covid low. op do as you feel.

61987741
>>61987552
Uphold (the crypto exchange) has a partnership checking account that is FDIC insured up to 2.25 million with deposits swept across 12 megabanks. It pays 3.75% APY paid out monthly which for you is almost $2k in annual interest just for holding with free ability to buy move to exchanges or brokerages to buy and sell and spend etc. with no lockups like a savings account that likely pays less. Most banks are paying literally .01% on deposits and are more exposed to systemic banking risk. Additionally, Uphold offers 2 or 4% (depending on what card you choose, the 2% is free but the 4% costs $100 per year) in XRP paid back on what you spend from your USD account, which is really nicely segmented from your interest account so you only want to keep in your other USD account what you intend to spend imminently and you can move between them in literally 10 seconds at a moment's notice. If you choose to use the account to deposit your paycheck you get another 4% in XRP, which is literally just like getting a 4% raise instantly and a 2-4% cut to expenses (other than credit card bills). So you get 3.75% on deposits held, 4% on deposited paycheck and 2-4% on what you spend. So far the most competitive way to hold cash I have seen. You could buy STRC and earn the divi payment as a cash position but if everything crashes crypto will also and you are massively risking your money by doing so. Hold strong cash and buy the real bottom.

61987746
>>61987738
People say every possible position every possible time. You fixating on some of them to think what you want is not significant. We haven't even begun to see the real fallout of the private credit crash or the backlog energy experience from the oil situation etc. You are literally comparing the beginning of inklings of drop to the time the largest fiscal stimulus package (with zero reserve requirement enacted) in US history. Lol

61987779
>>61987746
shut the fuck up nigger

61987939
>>61987779
You are low IQ and feel entitled to automatic rightness because your low threshold for parsing data and high expectation for cheap reward have hollowed you out into a petrified nothing. Become a real person again.

61987951
>>61987552
Just keep DCAing. Don't lump-sum your cash unless there is a 20% or higher crash in the market.

Make sure you have your 6 month - 1 year cash emergency fund.

61987971
>>61987552
>What if the market crashes?
you'll get your money back in about 10 years

61987986
keep buying voo during red days. i dont understand why people holds cash in the year 2026

61988212
Lump sum beats DCA (Proven), put it all in and forget about it, carry on with life and increasing your income.

61988226
>>61987576
this. I sold 1/3 of my portfolio a few weeks ago and thats my plan. just dont buy anything stupid, go with spy or whatever.

61988343
>>61987746
>We haven't even begun to see the real fallout
People say that every fucking time and every fucking time they're almost always wrong.

>>61987638

61987651

61987654

61987661

61987662

61987665

61987697

61987703

61987713

61987739
>>61987713

61987780

item.title: NO MORE RATE CUTS — 73 replies — 13.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
NO MORE RATE CUTS

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-03-15 17:33 UTC

OP

FUUUUUUUUCK

Early replies (first 3)

61981919
*blue states

61981925
>>61981915
high oil prices are a good thing chuddie

61981931
>Live in red state
>3.71 for gas
Wow, that's soooo bad. A whole SEVENTY ONE extra cent! OOOOOO soooo bad!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61983606
>>61981915
im sick of winning bros

61983751
>>61981931
paying more money for the sake of fighting wars on behalf of israel is based, trad, redpilled, AND maga. you lost, trannie!!!!!

61983949
>>61983073
That's in California. I've paid more than $6/gal for gasoline in California and that was years ago Whenever people want to spin up outrage about gas prices, they report the prices from California. California has a pretty toxic combination of the highest gasoline taxes in the US and the most expensive oil and gas regulations in the US. e.g. California requires special California-only blends of gasoline made only in refineries based in California - which they're busily trying to regulate out of existence.

Gas prices have barely moved in the US as well.

61983954
>>61981919
Imagine thinking red and blue matter

61983961
>>61982994
I would unironically rather be unemployed than deal with the humiliation ritual that is public transport.

61984042
Bruuuuh

61984247
>>61981915
Only wagies buy gas since they always need to be somewhere, usually their cage but in the weekend they have to run errands and do some other shit to cope with their normie life so they keep driving.

61984330
>ThePatrioticBlonde

61984823
he's fifa certified, let's believe in him

61984832
>>61981915
Trunpflation

61984865
>>61981915
americattle demons deserve their suffering
they fell for yet another sand war for their masters in israel, and in a few years they will pretend like they weren't all for it (again)
every time americattle go broke and/or die it brings a smile to my face

61984963
>>61983398
California's blue state dominance has held for many, many years. Since the 50s. Texas? Texas is blue, have you seen the population? Red areas are done. You're a bit delulu.

61985141
>>61983082
Just like the school bus. I bet you wear a back pack too.

61985891
>>61981915
Im from Southern California this gas just like all the $8 gas pics you have been seeing are from heavily populated city areas that are only bought by retards who are always riding near EMPTY and can survive a mile drive away to buy cheaper
I did just get more gas at COSTCO 2 days ago and it was $4.99 a gallon
>>61983398
We had gas companies and refineries fleeing California and that has contributed to insane prices.
They bitch about how we need to save the planet but the poorfags are more focused on how the fuck are they supposed to pay rent and get gas.
That $10-30 extra per tank matters to the poor and its bullshit that they are suffering from extreme regulations.

61985929
>>61982994
>>61983082
The main problem is that most Americans have to drive to or from public transportation.
This was not an accident.
I do wonder if I've paid more owning a car versus if I'd just called a taxi whenever I needed a car and couldn't take public transit.

61985943
>>61985891
vpn switching faggot, now you're pretending to be from southern california instead of ohio with the same formatted double reply
kill yourself faggot and your poor deflections from the fact gas is rising dramatically across the entire usa to try and poorly excuse your orange idiot's direct actions that's causing this
pathetic bitch, all you do is larp

61985949
>>61983273
They make sure to keep you people happy because y'all have relatively easy geographic access to the nukes.

61985969
>>61985943

>Actual So Cal Resident here

I work for a “private” transportation company that’s funded by taxpayers. I get to take a Tesla home from work everyday and not afraid of anything. Oh and I went leveragemaxxing on OIL.

Get fucked peasants.

61985990
>>61983014
Rural America is dead and decaying with zero jobs. Majority of Americans are now living in urban and suburban areas. Suburban tags are the same as the city fags but with longer commutes. Oh and the le based suburbs are getting darker every year.

61986071
>>61985943
I'm still here. You're nuts bro...

61986079
you're still here on a fresh vpn

61986254
>>61982994
You're not considering the medical bills for stitching up your stab wounds.

61986273
Doesn't America export oil? How come the price is going up?

61986287
>>61985990
White people make up the majority of people who live in suburbs and White births are on the rise. Nice cuck fantasy though.

61986950
>>61981915
>NO MORE RATE CUTS
Rate cuts? Shit's go back up again, probably

>>61984330
Booba

61987017
>>61986273
Oil is traded internationally, so everyone pays the same price. That said, the US might introduce export controls if the war continues.

61987264
>all these replies mentioning that their gas prices haven't risen yet
just wait until the markets open tomorrow
maybe think about filling up now

61987307
>>61987017
>everyone pays the same price
No they don't lmao. Futures market isn't the same as ordering actual physical delivery of oil to your door

61987366
>>61981915
I would fucking kill to be able to fill up for that cheap ever again. Currently 10.45 per gallon in Norway and it will probably go up more.

61987525
>>61985943
>>61985969
You know that you can just check the gas prices online right?
https://www.costcogasguide.com/california/
Theres no reason to lie when any lie like this would be easily debunked
That being said I am using a vpn and the way I reply is not rare or special in any way.
I am not that anon you are being schizo about

item.title: Is BTC just a store of value or more? — 67 replies — 12.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is BTC just a store of value or more?

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-03-15 17:38 UTC

OP

People say BTC is boring since it’s not pumping. Is this all it’s gonna be or will it evolve further?

Early replies (first 3)

61967712
>>61964560
Bitcoin is dead

61967714
>>61967712
You mean crypto in general

61967717
>>61967712
So is Dawson

RIP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61973015
>>61972666
so what do you want?

61974112
>>61967733
Just buy the dip if you want

61974187
>>61964560
Crypto is dead because nobody cares about it anymore, it is not needed for anything and people have shifted their attention to things they actually need

61975187
>>61972948
yeah ethereum has most liquidity for sure but btc’s catching up. it partnered with ledger, integration not live yet, users will soon be able to approve BTC vault transactions securely on-device and keep custody

61975192
>>61964560
BTC was almost double price a few months ago
Attention spans at an ATL

61975193
>>61972451
Then wait for it to dump. Anything can happen tho.

61975253
>>61973013
nah not wrapped if you take the time to look it up. it stays native and can actually be put to work instead of just sitting there

61975277
>>61967738
Anon, that’s literally the point. Bitcoin’s value isn’t backed by a company or government, it’s backed by people agreeing it’s scarce and useful. Digital receipts don’t matter, adoption and network effect do.

61977174
>>61967782
I guess you will stick to sending remittances lol

61977180
>>61967712
give me all 21 million of them

61977934
>>61972505
Do you even have Bitcoin? Lol

61978026
>>61967712
BTC is more alive than ever, I'm staking it on babylon

61978191
>>61975277
does it have usecases now?

61978227
>>61978026
ngmi

61978229
>>61977180
everyone's dream

61978597
>>61964560
Anything can be a store of value if we manage to organize a secure and efficient system of exchange. Yes gold is in principle as arbitrary as Bitcoin, but what people don't realize is just how difficult it is to come up with a practical medium of exchange. You need something that is scarce, secure, liquid and stable, because yeah obviously you just want something that just acts as a reference for value and nothing more.The "issue" with modern currencies that lolberts and schizos usually complain about is the idea of having centralized entities in charge of creating and regulating the medium, i.e. central banks. Bitcoin was supposedly an alternative under the delusional assumption that people were going to adopt it massively because muh banks are le evil. Now this is the crazy thing, suppose we do come together and say well lets adopt crypto universally as a system, the stuff with the transfer times and security is not even a big issue if enough people participate ( instead of what we spend on current currency infrastructure which still costs we spend it on crypto infrastructure in some way so enough people are mining all the time), the thing is why the fuck would people agree to use Bitcoin if already most of it is hoarded by a handful of people? We are by fiat allowing these hoarders to control the economy even though we are perfectly capable of creating a new crypto network? Just because it was first even if the code and idea is perfectly reproducibly? Except we are painfully retarded (which we are lol), there is no fucking reason to adopt Bitcoin. It's literally as retarded as someone buying gold at a stupid price hoarding it and expecting people to still buy it at that price because eventually we will adopt gold as THE medium of exchange.

61978709
>>61978026
You’re doing the right thing anon. Curious to see how TBVs scale once live.

61978716
>>61972599
Hard to argue with that anon. BTC’s growth over the years is still one of the craziest things in finance.

61978790
>>61972672
You can’t eat gold either

61978818
>>61964560
you can't store value

61978828
>>61972666
>a github repo
>bitcoin is the node implementation
holy fucking illiteracy

61978884
>>61978790
Actually incorrect, you can eat gold flakes but it has no nutritional value.

61979499
>>61978191
Ofc anon. People use it for payments, some traditional companies take it as collateral, and BTCFi is slowly showing how it can actually earn yield while staying on Bitcoin.

61979505
>>61972928
Why are you thinking like this anon?

61979516
>>61972666
You're not even close to correct, but even if you were, if it works for Linux and mogs microslop so fucking hard, why not for money?

61980340
>>61978227
Why assume that when you don’t even know how it works anon? BTC having more use cases is a good thing, not just OGs holding it forever.

61980350
>>61967907
What do you think?

61983404
>>61972957
Let's ask him

61986027
>>61978818
What do you mean?

61987534
>>61978790
So being able to eat a commodity is the only thing that makes it valuable? What are you even saying?

Why do you retards reach for nonsense? The reason why gold and silver are money, is because they had value as a commodity before they became recognised as money. And no, their cake didn't come from some abstract financial mechanism that was just a Ponzi scheme like crypto is. If you bought all the gold, silver and copper in the world, the modern day tech simply could not be possible. The world was in the stone age or whatever before precious metals and technology. Prior to Bitcoin the world was working perfectly fine. If you had an the gold, silver and copper in the world, people would need that shit for modern technology, for electricity, for computers, if you have all the bitchcoin in the world, no one would even know or care, it doesn't affect anyone or anything because it's not even a real world commodity. It does nothing that can't be done better than gold, silver and copper, and it does nothing better than a crypto that was actually backed by Gold, silver and copper which places world limits on the currency itself because it's backed by something tangible.

Can't believe it still has to be spelled out for idiots, but if you read this and you still pretend that crypto is but a crypto scam where gamblers win at the expense of the majority, then yeah, you deserve crypto and I hope you put all your earnings into that.

item.title: did Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) really spent 40 years valued at pennies? — 5 replies — 12.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
did Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) really spent 40 years valued at pennies?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-03-15 22:31 UTC

OP

did Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) really spent 40 years valued at pennies?

Early replies (first 3)

61988517
back in those days Intel was king and K6 sucked ass I never ever seriously considered an AMD CPU until the late 2010's when they got good and released Ryzen

61988539
Back in those days we had to hand write our orders in fractions of 1/16th cents and hand deliver them to our brokers, it was a different time

61988547
>>61988460
yes because for most of that time it did not have a good product

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61988590
Thoughts on SanDisk and Adobe right now? SanDisk looks good and Adobes new yt adds are very catchy

61988601
>>61988460
Yes. Thankfully those days are over now that the US has discontinued the penny.
